data.title: Alphabet's Waymo on track to start mapping Tokyo streets
data.description:
Waymo, Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) autonomous driving unit, is deploying 25 manually-driven rides to collect road data in Tokyo next week, marking its first international test drive. 

"After months of collaboration, Waymo, Nihon Kotsu, and GO will begin mapping streets using our manually-driven cars with Waymo technology across seven Tokyo wards soon," the company announced. Nihon Kotsu is a Japanese taxi firm and Go is a local ride-hailing app.

The initial mapping phase will enable Waymo vehicles to gain insights on local roads, driving patterns, and navigating city traffic.

"It's important for us to understand the difference in driving environment, what makes the driving here unique," Nicole Gavel, Waymo's head of business development and strategic partnerships, told reporters at an event in Tokyo, as reported by Reuters.

"We're just starting with the testing, so that's where we're focused," she added. "Longer term, we would love to have a business here in Tokyo."

Waymo robotaxis currently operate in San Francisco, Phoenix, Los Angeles, and Austin, providing more than 200,000 fully autonomous paid trips each week. Waymo plans to expand the service to Atlanta, Miami, and Washington, D.C.

data.title: ChipMOS reports higher Q1 revenue, plans to 'adjust accordingly' if tariffs hit demand
data.description:
ChipMOS’ (NASDAQ:IMOS) revenue for the first quarter of 2025 was NT$5,532.3 million or $166.7 million, an increase of 2.1% from the first quarter of 2024.

Revenue for March was NT$2,031.6 million or $61.2 million, representing an increase of 15.7% from February, and an increase of 5.1% from March 2024.

The company said its increased revenue reflects continued improvements in certain end markets and customer inventory levels, along with a positive but lesser impact from a limited uptick in its turns business.

Separately, the company (NASDAQ:IMOS) continues to closely monitor the rapidly evolving tariff situation and plans to adjust accordingly to support customers based on their exposure to the U.S. market and if the tariffs result in a decrease in demand.

Source: Press Release

data.title: China is isolated after Trump’s tariff pause, Bill Ackman says
data.description:
Billionaire investor Bill Ackman, who earlier in the week issued a stark warning over the potential fallout from President Donald Trump's escalating tariff strategy, has now welcomed the President’s 90-day pause on most reciprocal tariffs.

Trump on Wednesday said he was issuing a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for more than 75 countries, excluding China, that had reached out for negotiations. He also raised the tariff charged to China by the U.S. to 125%, by citing the country’s “lack of respect for global markets”.

Ackman, while praising President Trump’s decision, said the country now has a clearer sense of “who our preferred trading partners are and who the problems are,” singling out China as a “bad actor.”

In a post on X, the Pershing Square Capital Management CEO said China is now isolated and our other trading partners are lining up to make deals, adding that “Time is not China's friend as every U.S. corporation with a supply chain based in China is seeking to move it to countries that are likely to make favorable tariff deals with the United States.”

Meanwhile, China's own 84% retaliatory tariffs on U.S. imports took effect Thursday, intensifying the trade war between the world's two largest economies. China’s foreign ministry said today that Beijing was not interested in a fight “but will not fear if the United States continues its tariff threats.”

“The US cause doesn’t win the support of the people and will end in failure,” a ministry spokesperson, Lin Jian, said at a regular press conference.

China ETFs: (KWEB), (PGJ), (FXI), (ASHR), (YIN), (YANG), (GXC), (FXP), (KBA), (CXSE), (CNYA), (CHAU), (FLCH), (YXI), (FCA), (KALL), (JCHI)

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: China's purchase of US oil likely heading towards zero; JPM stays bullish on global copper miners
data.description:
Oil flows from the United States to China in the early months of this year have reportedly added up to roughly 1% of the country's imports, amid growing tariffs and trade risks.

Oil shipments from the world’s biggest producer to the largest importer have been on the decline for much of 2025, after climbing in recent years, as President Donald Trump’s successive rounds of tariffs heap pressure on the domestic refining sector.

“With China imposing 84% tariffs on goods from the US, the cost of US crude would be almost double — $51 a barrel more expensive, based on $61 WTI,” Ivan Mathews, head of APAC analysis for Vortexa told Bloomberg. “This makes running US crude uneconomical for Chinese refiners.”

US crude imports to China will “likely dwindle to zero in the coming months if the current tariff levels stay,” he added.

Brent prices (CO1:COM) fell 2.6% to $63.78/bbl by 6 am ET, WTI (CL1:COM) down 2.8% to $60.64/bbl.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Meanwhile, in the base metals space, JPM Commodities Research still sees the global copper market deficit widening to ~3Mt by 2030E and, as such, remains bullish on the global copper miners long-term.

JPM sticks to its view, although macro uncertainty could continue to weigh on mining equities near-term, adding that new leaching technologies could add ~590ktpa (~2% of global supply) to the market by 2030E, but is likely insufficient to fill the long-term market deficit.

In EMEA, the Bank prefers Lundin Mining (LUN:CA) and Antofagasta (OTC:ANFGF) but stays 'Underweight' on Boliden (OTCPK:BDNNY). 

In the Americas, it recently upgraded Freeport and expects FCX (FCX) to deliver material volume growth from new leaching technologies near-term.

It also rates Teck Resources (TECK) Overweight, but Southern Copper (SCCO) Neutral. 

Among the global diversified miners, it prefers Rio Tinto (RIO) given its ~30% copper volume growth to 2028E.

ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)

data.title: Stock index futures fall, yields also lower after Wall Street rally driven by trade relief fades
data.description:
Wall Street's rally on Wednesday might have run out of steam with stock index futures down on Thursday, as tariff concerns continued to dampen sentiment, while investors turned their attention to the latest inflation report. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -2.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -2.4%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 7 basis points to 4.29%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 8 basis points to 3.85%.

It was nothing short of a historic day for markets on Wednesday, as U.S. President Donald Trump's pause on reciprocal tariffs against most countries except China sent Wall Street to its best day since the financial crisis of 2008. The S&P 500 rocketed +9.52%; the Nasdaq Composite exploded +12.16%, and the Dow surged +7.87% on Wednesday. 

"Market turmoil turned into elation yesterday as Trump announced a 90 day pause on reciprocal tariffs for countries other than China, which marked a sizable backing off from last week’s tariff escalation," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Trump on Wednesday said he would issue a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for more than 75 countries who had reached out to the U.S. for trade negotiations.

Perhaps most crucially, we are currently still on course for a disorderly economic decoupling between the world’s two largest economies, with no immediate signs of either the U.S. or China backing down, Sidorov said.

Trump also upped the tariff rate against China to 125%. 

Recession risk in the US is lower than yesterday but remains high, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Traders will now keep their eyes on the March consumer inflation report, which will land before the bell. 

The March core CPI is expected to rise 0.3% in March, compared with the 0.2% increase in February, according to the consensus of economists. On a Y/Y basis, core CPI is expected to rise 3.0%, slowing from the 3.1% increase in the previous month.

The headline CPI number is expected to edge up 0.1%, slowing from the 0.2% increase in February. On a Y/Y basis, that would come to +2.6% vs. +2.8% in the prior month.

We get March consumer price inflation, but this reflects the before times. Some companies may raise prices in anticipation of taxes, but this is more likely in producer prices (retailers’ profit-led inflation depends on consumers knowing tariffs are in place), Donovan added. 

The initial jobless claims and the Fed balance sheet will also land on Thursday. The former is expected to rise to 223K.

data.title: Topgolf Callaway Brands to sell Jack Wolfskin business
data.description:
• Topgolf Callaway Brands (NYSE:MODG) said it will sell its Jack Wolfskin business to ANTA Sports for a base price of $290 million in cash.
  • The deal is expected to close in the late second quarter or early third quarter of 2025.
  • "This sale will allow us to increase our focus and optimize our resources on our core business. Importantly, the proceeds will further enhance our balance sheet and liquidity, reinforcing our financial flexibility ahead of our planned separation of Topgolf from our core operations," said CEO, Chip Brewer.

data.title: Four Corners Property Trust to acquire automotive service property
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT) said it has acquired an automotive service property leased to a national operator for $5.3 million.
  • The property is located in Arkansas and under a corporate long-term triple net lease.
  • The deal was priced at a 6.8% cap rate on rent as of the closing date and exclusive of transaction costs.
  • Shares +1.2% in premarket trade.

data.title: Google reiterates $75B spending plan for data centers amid tariff woes
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) CEO Sundar Pichai reaffirmed the company pledge to invest about $75B to build out data center infrastructure.

The comments come at a time when investors are concerned about the impact of the new U.S. tariffs.

"In 2025, we plan to invest around $75B in total capex. This investment will be directed towards our servers and data centers, which include powering our AI compute and cloud business," said Pichai at the Google Cloud Next Conference 2025 on Wednesday. So this will greatly benefit our customers, like all of you.

Pichai also said that the company was making Google's global private network available to enterprises around the world. The company calls is Cloud Wide Area Network, or WAN.

"Cloud WAN leverages Google's planet scale network. It's optimized for application performance and delivers over 40% faster performance, while reducing cost of ownership by up to 40%," said Pichai. He noted that it will be available to all Google cloud customers later this month.

U.S. companies, including Alphabet's Google, Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) have already spent billions in their last quarters, and plan a similar approach for 2025. 

Alphabet's capital expenditure, or CapEx, in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $14B, mainly reflecting investments in its technical infrastructure, with the largest component being investment in servers, followed by data centers, to help growth its business across Google Services, Google Cloud, and Google DeepMind, Alphabet's CFO Anat Ashkenazi had noted in February.

On Wednesday, President Donald Trump said he would temporarily lower the high tariffs he imposed on dozens of countries, while increasing tariff pressure on China.

Sachin Gupta, vice president and general manager for Google Cloud's infrastructure unit, told Reuters that the cost of importing hardware may rise but customer demand continued to necessitate the increased investment.

"We're all processing what's happening with tariffs," said Gupta as per the report.

Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: EU said to weigh buying more U.S. gas, but green transition still in focus
data.description:
The European Union is considering buying more U.S. liquefied natural gas to assuage President Donald Trump, even as it continues to target higher renewable energy capacity, the bloc's energy commissioner told the Financial Times. 

Trump has said the EU needs to fix its $350B trade deficit with the U.S. to get tariff relief. "One of the ways that can disappear easily is they're gonna have to buy our energy from us… they can buy it, we can knock off $350B in one week."

"There is potential for us to buy more LNG from the U.S., but of course, it needs to be on conditions that are also in line with our (green) transition," Dan Jørgensen, European commissioner for energy, told FT.

The EU is targeting to install record renewable energy capacity this year, which Jørgensen is essential in light of high energy prices and the bloc's reliance on Russian fossil fuels. "We have spent more money buying fossil fuels from Russia since 2022 than we have given in aid to Ukraine." 

But the EU's renewable energy industry has been challenged by red tape and poor grid connections. Jørgensen said the bloc wants to simplify rules, adding that average wait times for permits should be cut down from 5-7 years to just six months.

data.title: Fortuna reports production of 103,459 gold equivalent ounces in Q1, reiterates FY outlook
data.description:
• Fortuna Mining (NYSE:FSM) Thursday reported Q1 gold production of 91,893 ounces, compared to 89,678 oz in the year-ago period and 95,993 oz in Q4.
  • Reports gold equivalent production of 103,459 ounces, compared to 112,543 Au Eq oz (GEOs) a year ago.
  • Fortuna reiterates its 2025 annual production guidance range of 334,000 to 373,000 ounces of gold and 0.9 to 1.0 million ounces of silver or between 380,000 and 422,000 GEOs, including lead and zinc by-products.
  • The Lindero leach pad expansion project was completed in Q1 and delivered to operations on time and within budget at a total cost of $51.8 million. The expansion provides sufficient capacity for an additional 10 years of production.
  • Source: Press Release
